intro:
There is no information available about a plant called Asterocephalus succisa. However, I can provide you with a paragraph about the plant Succisa pratensis (Devil's-bit scabious or Succisa) which is sometimes confused with Asterocephalus succisa.

Succisa pratensis is a perennial plant in the honeysuckle family, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Succisa pratensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Herbal preparations made with Succisa pratensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and skin conditions.
